title: Must / Cannot — relationship criteria
html:
Must / Cannot — relationship criteria

Source: 2023-07-26 Apple Note. A two-column relationship-criteria sketch — what a partner must do and what they cannot be — written 1 week before the Jo breakup. The timing is suggestive: Yahir was already drafting his next-relationship rubric while still inside the current one.

TL;DR

  • MUST: be generous and grateful; say bless you and thank you; show affirmations of love; make me better through thinking in different positive manners
  • CANNOT: have nothing going for her; be unhygienic; be inconsiderate
  • The list is values + manners + ambition — not appearance, not chemistry, not income
  • Written 7 days before the Jo breakup — operating as a forward-looking rubric, not a postmortem
  • "Make me better through thinking in different positive manners" is the most distinctive line — Yahir wants a partner who changes how he thinks, not just supports him. Predicts the Emily arc's later "values misalignment" framing where intellectual co-evolution is the test that fails.

Pattern observed

The "cannot have nothing going for her" line is harsh on the page but consistent — Yahir picks partners with their own things going on (Jo, Sam, Emily all had distinct careers/scenes). The rubric isn't aspirational, it's descriptive of who he actually picks. The criterion that does keep failing is the "make me think in different positive manners" one — partners who reinforce him vs. expand him.

Open questions

  • Does this rubric ever appear again later in the corpus? Worth densify-checking against the Sam and Emily summaries.
  • The 7-day proximity to the Jo breakup — was this written as the breakup was already crystallizing internally?
